incident_description: AI-generated member insight deck included fabricated statistic claiming "40% of Thrive members are parents" — no source data exists for this claim. Stat was presented to leadership before being caught.
resolution: Removed from deck; manual fact-check added to creative review process
policy_gap_identified: No AI output review policy for externally-shared materials

incident_description: Claude provided case citations during contract research that appeared legitimate but referenced non-existent court opinions. Associate spent 2 hours verifying before discovering fabrication.
resolution: Mandatory Westlaw verification step added for all AI-assisted legal research
policy_gap_identified: No formal policy requiring verification of AI-generated legal citations

incident_description: Team explored automating bank file reconciliation but project was blocked by legal review — bank files contain sensitive financial data and SOX controls require documented human review chain.
resolution: Project paused pending SOX-compliant AI workflow design
policy_gap_identified: No framework for AI use in SOX-controlled financial processes

incident_description: Supply planning analyst submitted AI-generated demand forecast to leadership without disclosing AI assistance or reviewing the underlying assumptions. Forecast was 12% off actual.
resolution: Manager review added to forecast approval workflow; verbal policy on AI disclosure
policy_gap_identified: No company-wide AI disclosure or attribution requirements

incident_description: Sierra chatbot provided incorrect return policy information to 23 members over a 4-hour window after a policy update wasn't synced to Sierra's knowledge base.
resolution: Emergency knowledge base update; affected members contacted with corrections
policy_gap_identified: No SLA or process for syncing policy changes to AI systems

incident_description: Developer used Claude to generate infrastructure configuration that inadvertently exposed an internal staging API endpoint. Caught in code review before deployment.
resolution: Code review caught the issue; no production impact
policy_gap_identified: No AI-specific security review checklist for generated infrastructure code

incident_description: Creative team used 4 different AI image generation tools in one week for a single campaign — inconsistent brand aesthetics and $2K in redundant tool subscriptions.
resolution: Creative director consolidated to 2 approved tools for the team
policy_gap_identified: No centralized AI tool approval or procurement process

incident_description: Paralegal used Claude to draft a vendor agreement. AI included a non-standard liability clause that would have exposed Thrive to uncapped damages. Caught by attorney review.
resolution: All AI-drafted contracts now require attorney review before sending
policy_gap_identified: No policy on AI use in contract drafting — review was informal

incident_description: Covey AI screening tool rejected 3 qualified candidates based on resume formatting rather than qualifications. Discovered when hiring manager asked why strong referrals weren't progressing.
resolution: Manual review added for all AI-rejected candidates from referral pipeline
policy_gap_identified: No audit process for AI-assisted hiring decisions

incident_description: Analyst used Gemini (free tier) to analyze quarterly revenue data by pasting it into the consumer interface. Data included pre-earnings revenue figures.
resolution: Immediate policy memo prohibiting use of consumer AI tools for financial data
policy_gap_identified: No policy distinguishing enterprise AI tools from consumer/free tools for sensitive data

incident_description: AI-generated email campaign copy contained a health claim about a supplement product that hadn't been FDA-reviewed. Caught by compliance before send.
resolution: Health claims review checklist added to AI content workflow
policy_gap_identified: No AI content compliance checklist for regulated product categories

incident_description: Automated offboarding script revoked access for a contractor who was supposed to be extended. Trigger data from People Ops was 2 days stale.
resolution: Added 48-hour confirmation hold before executing AI-triggered access changes
policy_gap_identified: No human-in-the-loop requirement for AI-triggered access management

incident_description: QA team found that 8% of Sierra AI responses to member complaints used inappropriately cheerful tone when members described serious product quality issues (allergic reactions).
resolution: Sierra tone calibration updated; added sentiment-aware response routing
policy_gap_identified: No regular tone/empathy audit cadence for AI customer interactions

incident_description: PM used Claude to generate competitive analysis that included outdated pricing from competitors (6+ months old). Shared with executive team as current data.
resolution: Added date-verification step to competitive intelligence workflow
policy_gap_identified: No policy on verifying currency of AI-generated market data

incident_description: Claude Enterprise usage hit $28K in March — projected to reach $360K/yr. No budget approval exists for this scaling. Individual teams adding seats without central oversight.
resolution: CFO requested immediate spend review; temporary freeze on new seat additions
policy_gap_identified: No AI tool spend approval thresholds or central procurement for AI tools
